chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to the public repository containing the Ranger code: https://github.com/DependableSystemsLab/Ranger.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation uses TensorFlow (a software framework) and TensorFI (an FI tool for TensorFlow-based programs). While it tests on real-world datasets (like driving frames), the core fault injection and testing are performed within a simulated/software environment using the ML framework, not a physical hardware testbed.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: A clear, explicit section titled 'C. Fault Model' formally defines the scope of faults considered: hardware transient faults (soft errors) occurring in the processor's data path during inference, assuming at most one fault per execution, and specifically injecting single bit flips (with evaluation under multi-bit flip model).
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides a rigorous, data-driven analysis of trade-offs. It quantifies performance overhead using FLOPS (Table IV) showing an average of 0.530% increase, and it explicitly analyzes the accuracy vs. resilience trade-off by varying restriction bounds (Table V).

major_weakness: The experimental rigor is limited to software simulation/framework testing rather than physical hardware deployment.
